beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check plants and water the fiddle leaf fig
beats.reasoning: Monday morning routine; full energy and clear-headed start. Perfectionist tendency to tend houseplants first thing.

beats.activity: Drive to Kirkland workplace via I-5 North and I-405
beats.reasoning: Commute to transit agency office in Kirkland; early departure to avoid peak traffic and arrive calm for the work week.

beats.activity: Work on Light Rail maintenance coordination report and project planning
beats.reasoning: Monday morning work block; aware of Light Rail closure 9am-3pm (his agency's responsibility), so he's likely in office coordinating or reviewing closure impact rather than in the field.

beats.activity: Drive downtown Seattle to watch FIFA match (USA vs Wales) at a bar
beats.reasoning: High-energy moment: the 11am FIFA match is a real event, and Tomas values social time; he's heading downtown to catch it live at a bar rather than alone.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match, grab lunch and drinks with coworkers
beats.location_name: Pike Place Market Bar & Grill (Downtown Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Attending the 11am match live as a social outing; perfectionist Tomas doesn't skip work events, but he's also energized by the sunny weather and camaraderie of coworkers.
Return commute after lunch break; afternoon work block ahead.

beats.activity: Afternoon work: design review, Light Rail closure follow-up, and project documentation
beats.reasoning: Solid afternoon work block; Monday momentum and high-achieving nature keep him focused on transit agency deliverables.

beats.activity: Drive home from Kirkland to Wallingford
beats.reasoning: Evening commute; good sunny weather makes the drive pleasant, and he's ready to decompress at home.

beats.activity: Grocery shopping and errand run: pick up supplies for home brewing project
beats.reasoning: Quick neighborhood errand; Tomas uses sunny Monday evening to gather ingredients for weekend home brewing, fitting his perfectionist hobby.

beats.activity: Prepare dinner, organize home brewing supplies, water plants, relax on porch with notes
beats.reasoning: Wind-down evening at home; perfectionist preps brewing project, tends to plants, and enjoys the warm sunny weather on the porch before bed.
Full restorative sleep; Monday was productive and social, and Tomas is satisfied with the day's balance of work, social time, and personal projects.

diary:
Monday started like most do—coffee and checking on my fiddle leaf fig, which is finally bouncing back after last month's neglect. The drive to Kirkland was smooth, and I spent the morning buried in the Light Rail maintenance report. The closure between Westlake and University District is our baby, and I wanted the numbers tight before anyone asked questions.

But then the World Cup pulled me downtown. I couldn't resist. Ducked out around ten-thirty and made it to Pike Place Market Bar & Grill just as the USA-Wales match kicked off. Grabbed beers with a few folks from the office—turns out half of them had the same idea. The energy was electric, even if the game was a bit flat. Lunch was good, company was better.

Back to Kirkland for the afternoon grind: design reviews, closure follow-ups, documentation. Nothing glamorous, but necessary. By the time I got home, I was ready to decompress. Whole Foods for brewing supplies, then the porch with my notes and the evening light. Felt like I'd actually lived today, not just moved through it.
thought_process: I almost skipped the match—seemed irresponsible mid-project—but the weather was too perfect and the mood at the office was contagious. Sometimes you need those breaks, especially when you're managing something as complex as a transit closure. The morning work gave me permission to take the afternoon lighter; I'd put in the focused hours. The grocery run felt natural afterward, a way to transition from work-mode back to home. I've been wanting to get serious about home brewing, and today felt like the right moment to actually commit to it rather than just think about it. The fiddle leaf fig is a small thing, but watering it this morning and again tonight felt grounding—a reminder that I'm tending to things, not just reacting to them.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
